concepts.name: What is a Business / Organization — basics of enterprise and society
concepts.description: Introduce the idea of businesses and organizations: what they are, why they exist; concept of goods/services, basic roles (producer, consumer), simple supply & demand ideas; role of organizations in everyday life and economy.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Visualize simple economy: producers provide goods/services, consumers consume; show flows of goods & money; illustrate why organizations/businesses form.

learning_objectives: Define what a business or organization is and describe its purpose.
Explain difference between goods and services, producers and consumers.
Recognize that businesses exist to provide goods/services and satisfy needs.

concepts.name: Basic Economics: Supply, Demand & Markets (intro)
concepts.description: Introduce simple supply and demand dynamics: what supply/demand mean, how price emerges, how supply and demand curves shift, simple market equilibrium idea, effects of scarcity and consumer choices.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Allow user to manipulate supply & demand curves (shift supply/demand), observe effect on price and quantity, simulate shortage or surplus, basic market dynamics.

learning_objectives: Define supply, demand, and market price conceptually.
Simulate how shifts in supply or demand affect price and quantity.
Recognize effects of scarcity, surplus, and shortage on markets.

concepts.name: Money, Trade & Value — basics of exchange and barter to currency
concepts.description: Explain how trade originally worked (barter), problems with barter, how money/currency solves trade problems, concept of value, medium of exchange, basics of prices and exchange value.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Simulate barter exchanges between agents; show inefficiencies; then simulate trade using money/currency; illustrate how currency simplifies transactions and allows for value storage and exchange.

learning_objectives: Explain difference between barter and currency-based trade.
Understand why money/currency emerged historically as medium of exchange.
Recognize how currency enables trade and reflects value.

concepts.name: Roles & Jobs: what workers, employers and organizations do
concepts.description: Introduce concepts of work: employees, employers, roles/functions, organization structure at very simple level; what earning means; wages/salary, labor vs capital; how people contribute to organizations.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Create simple organization with roles, assign workers, simulate production process, show that workers get compensation, organization produces output — illustrate how work and wages connect.

learning_objectives: Define roles of worker and employer in an organization.
Understand basic concept of labor, wages and compensation.
Recognize that organizations coordinate work to produce goods/services.

concepts.name: Simple Business Models — how businesses make money
concepts.description: Basic idea of how businesses generate revenue: cost vs revenue, profit; simple model of buying resources, producing goods/services, selling to customers — concept of margin, cost, revenue, profit in intuitive sense.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Let user run a toy business: buy inputs at cost, transform into outputs, sell at price — compute revenue, cost, profit or loss; visualize profitability depending on price and cost structure.

learning_objectives: Understand basic cost, revenue, and profit concepts.
Simulate simple business operations and compute profit/loss.
Recognize how price, cost and volume affect business viability.

concepts.name: Market Structures & Competition: monopoly, oligopoly, perfect competition, monopolistic
concepts.description: Introduce different types of market structures — perfect competition, monopoly, oligopoly, monopolistic competition — what differentiates them: number of firms, barriers to entry, price vs quantity competition, basic implications for consumers and producers.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Simulate markets under different structures, allow user to change number of firms, entry barriers, cost structures; observe price, output, consumer surplus, producer surplus under each scenario.

learning_objectives: Describe various market structures and their characteristics.
Simulate how market structure affects price, output, competition, consumer/producer surplus.
Analyze implications of barriers to entry and market power on welfare and competition.

concepts.name: Costs, Revenue, Profit & Break-Even Analysis
concepts.description: Introduce cost structures (fixed vs variable costs), revenue, marginal cost and revenue, break-even point; basic accounting/finance view of a small business — understand how costs and volume affect profitability and decision-making.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Allow user to set cost parameters (fixed, variable), choose price, vary production volume, compute revenue, cost, profit/loss; plot cost/revenue curves and identify break-even volume.

learning_objectives: Differentiate fixed vs variable costs, and compute total cost for given volume.
Calculate revenue, marginal cost/revenue, profit or loss for various volumes.
Perform break-even analysis and interpret results for business decisions.

concepts.name: Basic Organizational Structure & Management: departments, roles, coordination
concepts.description: Explain how organizations/businesses are structured internally: departments (e.g. production, sales, finance), roles, hierarchy vs flat, coordination and communication, basic idea of management and leadership functions in organizations.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Let user build an organization: assign departments & roles, define workflows, simulate tasks and communication flows, observe how structure impacts efficiency, output, and coordination overhead.

learning_objectives: Define different parts of an organization and their functions.
Understand how organizational structure and roles affect workflow and productivity.
Simulate how communication and coordination impact organizational performance.

concepts.name: Introduction to Marketing & Demand — how businesses reach customers
concepts.description: Describe concept of marketing, demand generation, customers, basic marketing tools (advertising, product design, pricing), supply-demand interplay from business perspective — how businesses influence demand and respond to consumer preferences.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Let user simulate a market where a business modifies product attributes, price, advertising investment; observe changes in demand, revenue, and profit; explore how marketing influences demand and sales.

learning_objectives: Explain basic marketing concepts and their role in influencing demand.
Simulate impact of pricing, advertising, and product attributes on demand and revenue.
Understand business strategies to attract customers and compete in market.

concepts.name: Business Strategy & Competitive Advantage
concepts.description: Study of how firms develop strategy to gain competitive advantage: SWOT, core competencies, differentiation vs cost leadership, value proposition, strategic positioning, dynamic competition over time, strategic decision-making.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Let user run a virtual firm in competitive environment, choose strategic levers (price, cost, differentiation, marketing), simulate market over multiple time periods, track market share, profits, competitor reactions; test different strategies.

learning_objectives: Formulate strategic decisions to achieve competitive advantage.
Analyze trade-offs between cost, quality, differentiation, and market share.
Simulate effect of strategic choices over time in a competitive environment.

concepts.name: Organizational Behavior & Leadership: motivation, culture, structure, change
concepts.description: Understand how human behavior, culture, organizational structure and leadership influence performance: motivation theories, group dynamics, corporate culture, organizational change, leadership styles, communication, incentives, teamwork and organizational effectiveness.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Simulate an organization with agents (employees), define culture, leadership style, incentives, communication flows; run simulation over time to observe productivity, turnover, morale, conflict; test how changes in structure or policies influence outcomes.

learning_objectives: Explain how organizational structure and culture affect performance and employee behavior.
Simulate team dynamics, motivation, leadership effects, and organizational change.
Use incentive and communication models to influence organizational outcomes.

concepts.name: Finance & Accounting Fundamentals for Firms
concepts.description: Core financial concepts within firms: balance sheets, income statements, cash flow, assets/liabilities, capital structure, investment vs cost, risk & return, basic financial decision making for businesses.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Allow user to manage a virtual firm’s finances: track assets/liabilities, cash flow, simulate investment decisions, compute profit/loss, analyze risk/return, see long-term financial health under different scenarios.

learning_objectives: Interpret financial statements: balance sheet, income statement, cash flow.
Make business decisions based on financial data (investment, debt, capital structure).
Evaluate business risk and return under different financial strategies.

concepts.name: Macro-economics Basics: aggregate economy, supply/demand, inflation, GDP, unemployment
concepts.description: Introduction to macro-economic concepts: gross domestic product (GDP), aggregate supply and demand, inflation/deflation, unemployment, business cycles, role of government, money supply, fiscal/monetary policy at a high-level view.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Simulate a simplified national economy: production, consumption, inflation, employment; allow user to adjust policies (tax, government spending, money supply) and observe macroeconomic indicators over time.

learning_objectives: Understand macroeconomic indicators (GDP, inflation, unemployment) and how they interact.
Simulate effects of fiscal and monetary policy on economy.
Analyze trade-offs and dynamics of macroeconomic policy over time.

concepts.name: Organizational Theory & Institutional Analysis
concepts.description: Advanced study of how organizations behave, evolve, and interact with environment: institutional theory, organizational ecology, networks, organizational lifecycle, governance, regulation, organizational change and adaptation over time.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Allow user to build organizations or a population of organizations, define institutional rules/regulations/environment, simulate lifecycle (birth, growth, decline), interactions, mergers/acquisitions, regulatory impact, competition/co-operation, and observe systemic patterns over time.

learning_objectives: Analyze organizational behavior and adaptation under varying institutional and environmental pressures.
Simulate organizational lifecycle and interactions among organizations (competition, cooperation, regulation).
Investigate governance, regulation and structure impacts on organizational success and survival.

concepts.name: Market Dynamics, Business Cycles & Economic Modeling (macro + micro interplay)
concepts.description: Use computational economic models to simulate both micro-level firm/market behavior and macro-level economy — supply/demand, business cycles, inflation/unemployment, shock response, policy effects, feedback loops between firms, consumers, and macroeconomic aggregate.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Allow user to build a simulated economy: define agents (firms, consumers, government), markets, policies; run over time to observe cycles, crises, inflation/deflation, employment dynamics, and study effects of external shocks or policy interventions.

learning_objectives: Model micro- and macro-economic interactions and emergent behavior.
Simulate business cycles, shocks, policy interventions, and their effect on economy indicators.
Analyze resilience, stability, and systemic risk in economies under different scenarios.

concepts.name: Behavioral Economics & Organizational Psychology: decision making, biases, incentives
concepts.description: Study human behavior and decision-making in economics and organizations: psychological biases, incentives, bounded rationality, game theory basics, cooperation vs competition, organizational culture impact on decision, behavioral modeling within firms and markets.

simulation_metadata.purpose: Let user define agents with preferences, biases, incentives; simulate decision-making in markets or organizational contexts; study outcomes like cooperation, competition, market inefficiencies, firm behavior under behavioral constraints.

learning_objectives: Model decision-making under bounded rationality and biases.
Simulate behavior of individuals and groups in markets or organizations under varying incentives.
Analyze outcomes of behavioral biases on efficiency, cooperation, competition, and organizational performance.
